topic: sparkling base wine oxidation and quality loss

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A delicate, high-acid base wine is exposed to excessive oxygen before the sparkling-wine process is complete.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Oxygen consumes protective compounds and accelerates the loss and transformation of fresh primary aromas.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The finished sparkling wine can show diminished freshness, bruised or stale aromas, and lower overall quality.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The finished sparkling wine can show diminished freshness, bruised or stale aromas, and lower overall quality.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
